body_text:
29	Using
MemberDirect
脗庐
Business (MDB) / Forge Commercial to Create Wires
29.1	Overview of Using MDB / Forge Commercial to Create Wires
The optional wires feature in MDB or Forge Commercial (FX or USD/CAD option) allows business customers to create domestic and international wires using business banking. After business users create and authorize an outgoing wire in MDB or Forge Commercial, it芒聙聶s sent to the Wires application for processing. Authorized users at your financial institution review and process business customer芒聙聶s wires in the Wires application as required.
Note:	Wires are monitored in Central 1芒聙聶s Enterprise Fraud Management (EFM) system. Outgoing wires that are alerted as potential fraud are managed in EFM. Wires doesn芒聙聶t notify Wires customers when wires are held in EFM as potential fraud. See Section 14.5, Outgoing Wires Monitored in Enterprise Fraud Management (EFM), and the Enterprise Fraud Management Part for more information.
The number of financial institution approvals required is based on authorization limits set in MTS. See Section 29.3, Overview of MTS Branch Management for MDB / Forge Commercial Wires. Central 1 forwards wires to the beneficiary芒聙聶s financial institution through domestic and international networks leveraging the existing Wires application infrastructure.
Note:	Business customers can芒聙聶t access the Wires application directly.
To use the MDB or Forge Commercial Wires feature your financial institution must:
Create one business customer MTS User ID (username) for each business that will use the wires feature. The business customer芒聙聶s MTS User ID acts as proxy to integrate the wires feature in MDB or Forge Commercial with the Wires application. See Section 29.5, Creating an MDB / Forge Commercial Business Customer User ID in MTS.
Assign the business customer芒聙聶s profile access to the wires function via the Business User Administration Application (BUA). See the Commercial FX Wires 芒聙聯 User Guide for Business User Administration (BUA) for procedures.
Enter the business customer芒聙聶s MTS User ID (username) and home transit number (MTS transit used for MDB or Forge Commercial Wires) in their profile in the Business User Administration (BUA) application and configure business user settings. See the Commercial FX Wires 芒聙聯 User Guide for Business User Administration (BUA) for procedures.
Verify that appropriate authorization limits for the transit used for MDB or Forge Commercial Wires are set up in MTS. See Section 29.3, Overview of MTS Branch Management for MDB / Forge Commercial Wires.
Provide the business user(s) with a 2-Step Security token. See the 2-Step Security for Business User Administration (BUA) 芒聙聯 User Guide for procedures. To use the MDB or Forge Commercial Wires function, the business user must log in to business banking using two-factor authentication.
Ensure authorized Wires Users at your financial institution have the appropriate permissions and sufficient approval limits to review and approve business customer wires (as required). The users must have access to the transit used for MDB or Forge Commercial wires. See Chapter 8, Wires User Management.
Related Information
Commercial FX Wires 芒聙聯 User Guide for Business User Administration (BUA)
2-Step Security for Business User Administration (BUA) 芒聙聯 User Guide
29.2	Financial Institution Roles for MDB / Forge Commercial Wires
The following financial institution roles are required for MDB or Forge Commercial Wires.
User Management Security Officers
Manage Client Centre User IDs in User Management and grant access to the following applications. See the User Management Part for procedures.
芒聢聮
MTS
芒聙聯 Assign this application to MTS Administrators managing branch settings, business customer MTS user IDs, Wires application MTS user IDs, and other MTS Administrators.
芒聢聮
Business User Administration (BUA)
芒聙聯 Assign this application to users managing business customer profile settings and their access to the wire function.
芒聢聮
The Wires application
芒聙聯 Assign this application to staff processing your business customer芒聙聶s wires (e.g., reviewing, approving wires).
Manage 2-Step Security tokens if required for the role. See the 2-Step Security Part for procedures.
MTS Administrators
Manage the following business wire settings in MTS:
芒聢聮
MTS Branch Settings 芒聙聯
The amount at which wires created in MDB / Forge Commercial require authorization in the Wires application in the Level 1 and Level 2 Authorization fields. Limits are set up under the transit(s) used to process business customer wires. See Chapter 9, MTS Branch Management for Wires, for procedures.
芒聢聮
MTS User IDs
for your business customers
芒聙聯 Creates one MTS User ID per business. Authorized BUA users will enter the business customer芒聙聶s MTS User ID in BUA to facilitate wires created in MDB / Forge Commercial. MTS User IDs are set up under the transit(s) used to process business customer wires. See Section 29.5, Creating an MDB / Forge Commercial Business Customer User ID in MTS.
芒聢聮
MTS User IDs
for your Wires application users
芒聙聯 Note: Your User Management Security Officer must set up the user芒聙聶s Client Centre User ID and assign them access to the Wires application before creating their MTS User ID. See Section 8.6, Creating a Wires User in MTS.
芒聢聮
Other MTS Administrators 芒聙聯
See Chapter 7, MTS Administrator Management for Wires, for procedures.
Business User Administration (BUA) Financial Institution Administrators
Manage the business customer芒聙聶s access to the MDB / Forge Commercial wire feature as follows:
芒聢聮
Enter the business customer芒聙聶s MTS user ID (1 per business) in the business customer芒聙聶s BUA profile. See the Commercial FX Wires 芒聙聯 User Guide for Business User Administration (BUA).
芒聢聮
Manage the business customer芒聙聶s user limits. See the Commercial FX Wires 芒聙聯 User Guide for Business User Administration (BUA).
芒聢聮
Assign a 2-step security token to business users. See 2-Step Security for Business User Administration (BUA) User Guide.
Wires Application Users
Monitor, review, and process your business customer芒聙聶s outgoing wires and any wires the beneficiary芒聙聶s financial institution returns. See Section 29.10, Processing MDB / Forge Commercial Wires in the Wires Application, for more information.
Related Information
Commercial FX Wires 芒聙聯 User Guide for Business User Administration (BUA)
2-Step Security for Business User Administration (BUA) User Guide
29.3 	Overview of MTS Branch Management for MDB / Forge Commercial Wires
MTS Administrators manage the amount at which outgoing wires that your business customers create in MDB or Forge Commercial require your financial institution芒聙聶s approval in the Wires application. Central 1 strongly recommends that financial institutions review and approve all business customer wires. See Chapter 9, MTS Branch Management for Wires, for procedures.
Your financial institution芒聙聶s policies can include, but aren芒聙聶t limited to, the following:
Using a home transit number (MTS Branch) for business customer wires not used for in-branch wires so that
芒聢聮
Wires Users at your financial institution responsible for processing business customer wires can be assigned specifically to the transit(s) used for MDB or Forge Commercial wires,
芒聢聮
Wires Users can more easily research and create reports for business wires via Submitted Wires, and
芒聢聮
authorization limits can be set specifically for outgoing wires your business customers create in MDB or Forge Commercial without affecting outgoing wires created in-branch.
Setting the MTS Level 1 Authorization field to $1.00 for the home transit number(s) used to process wires your business customers create in MDB or Forge Commercial. This ensures that authorized users at your financial institution review and approve all business customer wires in the Wires application. While you can set your Level 1 Authorization field according to your financial institution芒聙聶s risk appetite, it芒聙聶s important to note that wire amounts equal to or less than the value in the Level 1 Authorization field are sent without your financial institution芒聙聶s review or approval.
Important:	The Level 1 Authorization field value doesn芒聙聶t apply to in-branch wires. All wires created in-branch require at least one approval. Your financial institution is responsible for reporting suspicious transactions of any amount to FINTRAC. The only way to review wires created in MDB or Forge Commercial is to ensure that they require approval in the Wires application.
Setting an appropriate MTS Level 2 Authorization field value to designate when a wire requires two approvals in the Wires application. If the same transit is used for MDB / Forge Commercial Wires, the amount entered applies to both in-branch and business banking wires.
29.4	Overview of Managing MDB / Forge Commercial Business Customer User IDs in MTS
A business customer芒聙聶s MTS User ID acts as a proxy to integrate the MDB or Forge Commercial wire feature with the Wires application. It also identifies outgoing wires created by businesses on MDB or Forge Commercial in the Wires application and assists with traces and queries.
An MTS Administrator creates one MTS User ID per business authorized to use the MDB or Forge Commercial wire feature. Don芒聙聶t create more than one MTS User per business customer. Although multiple business users may have access to the wire function, all wires from that business customer funnel through the one MTS User ID.
Important
:	Don芒聙聶t create a corresponding Client Centre User ID because business users don芒聙聶t log in through the Client Centre.
Your financial institution芒聙聶s policies can include, but aren芒聙聶t limited to:
Restricting access to the MDB or Forge Commercial wires function.
Setting up business customer MTS user IDs under a home transit number (MTS Transit) not used for in-branch wires.
芒聢聮
Authorization limits can then be set specifically for outgoing wires created in business banking without affecting outgoing wires created in-branch. See Section 29.3, Overview of MTS Branch Management for MDB / Forge Commercial Wires.
芒聢聮
Wires Users at your financial institution responsible for processing business customer wires can be assigned specifically to the transit(s) used for MDB / Forge Commercial wires. See Section 29.10, Processing MDB / Forge Commercial Wires in the Wires Application.
芒聢聮
Wires Users can more easily research and create reports for business wires via Submitted Wires by the transit. See Chapter 20, Searching for Wires and Exporting Wire Information.
29.5	Creating an MDB / Forge Commercial Business Customer User ID in MTS
An MTS Administrator creates an MDB or Forge Commercial business customer MTS user ID as follows:
Log in to MTS as described in Section 6.2, Logging in to MTS, then select
New
. See Figure 1.
Figure 1: Selecting 芒聙聹New芒聙聺
Complete the business customer芒聙聶s MTS User profile details as follows. See Figure 2.
芒聭
Login ID
芒聙聯
Enter the business customer芒聙聶s user ID. Use the following format: 芒聙聹mdb-business name芒聙聺 (e.g., mdb-joesflowers). The user ID must be in lowercase, contain no spaces, and doesn芒聙聶t exceed 20 characters.
芒聭隆
Home Transit
芒聙聯
Select the home transit number used for MDB or Forge Commercial wires from the drop-down menu.
Note:	Multiple branches display in the drop-down menu if other transit numbers are linked to your home transit number, and you have permission to access the linked branches.
芒聭垄
Suspended
芒聙聯
Select the check box to remove the checkmark and activate the profile.
芒聭拢
Contact Details
芒聙聯
Enter the business customer芒聙聶s contact name and information.
芒聭陇
Permissions 芒聙聯
Select
View Outgoing Transfers
and
Create Outgoing Transfers
in the Outbox Permission section. The other fields don芒聙聶t apply. Leave them blank.
Note:	There are no limits in MTS that apply to creating wires. Business wire limits are managed in BUA. See the Commercial FX Wires 芒聙聯 User Guide for Business User Administration (BUA) for more information.
Select
Submit
.
Figure 2: Creating an MTS User ID for MDB or Forge Commercial
Have another MTS Administrator authorize the change. See Section 29.8, Authorizing Changes to MDB / Forge Commercial Business Customer User Profiles in MTS.
The business customer芒聙聶s MTS User ID and home transit must be entered in the business customer芒聙聶s profile in BUA. See the Commercial FX Wires 芒聙聯 User Guide for Business User Administration (BUA) for more information.
Related Information
Commercial FX Wires 芒聙聯 User Guide for Business User Administration (BUA)
29.6	Modifying an MDB / Forge Commercial Business Customer User ID in MTS
An MTS Administrator modifies an MDB or Forge Commercial business customer芒聙聶s MTS User ID permissions or contact information as follows:
Note: 	The user ID and home transit number can芒聙聶t be changed.
Log in to MTS as described in Section 6.2, Logging in to MTS.
Select the branch used for MDB or Forge Commercial wires from the Transit Number drop-down menu, then select
Go
. See Figure 3.
Note:	Select
All
in the drop-down menu to view users for all branches.
Note:	Multiple branches display in the drop-down menu if other transit numbers are linked to your home transit number, and you have permission to access the linked branches.
Figure 3: Select Branch
Select the user ID of the MDB or Forge Commercial business customer user you want to modify. See Figure 4
Figure 4: Selecting a Wires User
Tip
:	If there is a pending change to the business customer芒聙聶s user profile, it won芒聙聶t appear in the User queue. Authorize or cancel the pending change before proceeding. See Section 29.8, Authorizing Changes to MDB / Forge Commercial Business Customer User Profiles in MTS.
Modify the user as required. See Section 29.5, Creating an MDB / Forge Commercial Business Customer User ID in MTS, for a description of each field.
Select
Update
. See Figure 5.
Figure 5: Modify User
Have another MTS Administrator authorize the change. See Section 29.8, Authorizing Changes to MDB / Forge Commercial Business Customer User Profiles in MTS.
29.7	Deleting an MDB / Forge Commercial Business Customer User ID in MTS
An MTS Administrator deletes an MDB or Forge Commercial business customer芒聙聶s MTS user ID as follows:
Log in to MTS as described in Section 6.2, Logging in to MTS.
Select a branch from the transit number drop-down menu, then select
Go
. See Figure 6.
Note:	Select
All
in the drop-down menu to view users for all branches.
Note:	Multiple branches display in the drop-down menu if other transit numbers are linked to your home transit number, and you have permission to access the linked branches.
Figure 6: Selecting a Branch
Select the user ID of the MDB or Forge Commercial business customer you want to delete. See Figure 7.
Figure 7: Selecting User ID
Tip
:	If there is a pending change to the business customer芒聙聶s user profile, it won芒聙聶t appear in the User queue. Authorize or cancel the pending change before proceeding. See Section 29.8, Authorizing Changes to MDB / Forge Commercial Business Customer User Profiles in MTS.
Select
Delete
. See Figure 8.
Figure 8: Delete User
Have another MTS Administrator authorize the change via the Change List. See Section 29.8, Authorizing Changes to MDB / Forge Commercial Business Customer User Profiles in MTS.
Remove the business customer from BUA as required. See the Commercial FX Wires 芒聙聯 User Guide for Business User Administration (BUA) for procedures.
Related Information
Commercial FX Wires 芒聙聯 User Guide for Business User Administration (BUA)
29.8	Authorizing Changes to MDB / Forge Commercial Business Customer User Profiles in MTS
Authorization is required when an MDB or Forge Commercial business customer user ID is added, modified, or deleted. An MTS Administrator proceeds as follows to authorize a change:
Note:	See Section 7.4, Description of Columns in the MTS Change List Queue (Users and Administrators), for a description of each column.
Log in to MTS as described in Section 6.2, Logging in to MTS, and select
Change List
. See Figure 9.
Tip
:	The number next to 芒聙聹User Changes芒聙聺 reflects the number of unauthorized user and administrator changes.
Figure 9: Access Change List
Select the MTS user ID of the MDB or Forge Commercial business customer you want to authorize. See Figure 10.
Figure 10: Select User ID
Review the change and select
Authorize
. See Figure 11. Alternatively, select
Cancel
to delete the change.
Figure 11: Authorize Change
29.9	FINTRAC Reporting and PEP Determinations for Wires Created in MDB / Forge Commercial
Central 1 strongly recommends your financial institution reviews all wires created in MDB or Forge Commercial. You must comply with all regulatory requirements for business customer wires. This includes, but isn芒聙聶t limited to, PEP determination, record keeping, identification of the beneficiary, and FINTRAC reporting. Contact your compliance officer or FINTRAC for additional information. Subscribers to the Operations Manual Program can also refer to the PCMLTFA Compliance Part.
Your financial institution芒聙聶s policies can include:
Setting the MTS Level 1 Authorization field value to $1.00 for the home transit number(s) to which the MTS User IDs associated with MDB or Forge Commercial wires are assigned. This ensures that all outgoing wires created in MDB or Forge Commercial are reviewed and require financial institution approval in the Wires application. Your financial institution is responsible for reporting suspicious transactions of any amount to FINTRAC. The only way to review wires created in MDB or Forge Commercial is to ensure that they require approval in the Wires application.
Making a PEP determination on all MDB or Forge Commercial business customer users that are assigned access or authorization rights to the MDB or Forge Commercial wires function. Central聽1 recommends that a PEP determination be done during the set-up process.
Financial institutions that have enabled the Politically Exposed Person (PEP) and Head of International Organization (HIO) Determination Questions in the Wires application
If you want to record the answers to PEP/HIO determination questions for Very Large Wires ($100,000 Canadian equivalent or greater) created in MDB or Forge Commercial in the Wires application (and if applicable, the Wires Data Extract), an authorized Wires user must edit the wire. See Chapter 21, Editing an Outgoing Wire, for procedures. A Wires User that edits a wire can芒聙聶t approve it.
29.10	Processing
MDB / Forge Commercial Wires in the Wires Application
Authorized users at your financial institution review and approve your business customer芒聙聶s wires in the Wires application. The number of approvals required is based on your MTS authorization limits and the value of the wire. See Section 29.3, Overview of MTS Branch Management for MDB / Forge Commercial Wires.
Note:	Wires are monitored in Central 1芒聙聶s Enterprise Fraud Management (EFM) system. Outgoing wires that are alerted as potential fraud are managed in EFM. See Section 14.5, Outgoing Wires Monitored in Enterprise Fraud Management (EFM), and the Enterprise Fraud Management Part for more information.
Wire amounts less than or equal to the value in the MTS Level 1 Authorization field don芒聙聶t require financial institution approval in the Wires application. Wires that don芒聙聶t require your approval display in Submitted Wires in the Wires application.
Note:	Central 1 recommends that your financial institution reviews and approves all business customers wires.
Wire amounts greater than the value in the MTS Level 1 Authorization field, up to and including the value in the Level 2 Authorization field, require one financial institution approval in the Wires application.
Wire amounts greater than the value in the MTS Level 2 Authorization field require two financial institution approvals in the Wires application.
Business customers won芒聙聶t be aware of this approval requirement, so it芒聙聶s important that you regularly review pending outgoing wires in the Wires application.
Wires users responsible for reviewing and approving business customer wires must be assigned the following:
Access to the transit(s) used for MDB / Forge Commercial wires
The MTS 芒聙聹Authorize Outgoing Transfers芒聙聺 permission
Sufficient daily limit and/or transfer limits for approving business customer wires
A 2-step security token
Overview on Approving Outgoing Business Customer Wires
Wires created in MDB or Forge Commercial that require financial institution approval display in Pending Outgoing and the Submitted Wires. The Status column indicates the number of approvals required. See Figure 12. See Section 22.2, Approving Outgoing Wires, for procedures.
Figure 12: Number of Approvals Required
Overview on Editing Outgoing Business Customer Wires
Wires created in MDB or Forge Commercial that are pending financial institution approval can be edited in the Wires application. Only the user who edits a wire can edit it again. Users who edit a wire can芒聙聶t approve it. Editing a business customer芒聙聶s wire is optional. For example, if you want to record the answers to PEP/HIO determination questions for Very Large Wires ($100,000 Canadian equivalent or greater) created in MDB or Forge Commercial in the Wires application or enter additional payment details. See Chapter 21, Editing an Outgoing Wires, for procedures.
Note: 	Only the Sender, Beneficiary, Payment Details, Sender FI to Receiver FI Information, and Regulatory information can be edited. You can芒聙聶t edit the Sender FI Details, Receiver FI Details, Intermediary, Amount, Currency, or Fees fields.
Overview of Cancelling Outgoing Business Customer Wires
Wires created in MDB or Forge Commercial that are pending financial institution approval can be cancelled in the Wires application. Once a wire is approved, it can芒聙聶t be cancelled. See Chapter 23, Cancelling Outgoing Wires, for procedures.
Note:	The Wires application isn芒聙聶t connected to your banking system. Follow your internal procedures to settle with the sender.
Overview of Managing Returned Business Customer Wires
If the beneficiary financial institution returns a wire created in MDB or Forge Commercial wires, it displays in Pending Incoming in the Wires application as a 芒聙聹Return芒聙聺 type wire. See 芒聙聹Accepting a 芒聙聵Return芒聙聶 Type Incoming Wire芒聙聺 in Section 24.5, Accepting Incoming Wires, for procedures.
Note:	Regularly check pending incoming wires for returned wires.
END
